codeReview.summary: The feature adds reconciliation artifacts and basic tests, but the winner-selection logic is not aligned with the reconciliation contract for real DAGs: it selects from all completed/failed nodes and derives sequence precedence from static DAG order rather than terminal completion sequence. This can select an incorrect winner and persist an incorrect winner reason.

issues.description: Reconciliation candidates are built from every node with status completed/failed, which includes internal prerequisite nodes, not just conflicting terminal outcomes. In DAGs with dependencies this allows upstream nodes to incorrectly participate as winner candidates.

The 'earliest terminal sequence number' tie-break is computed from `terminalNodes.firstIndex` (DAG declaration order), not actual terminal completion sequence. This violates the precedence contract and can produce the wrong winner/reason when completion order differs from node declaration order.

Winner reason classification uses absolute thresholds (`> 0.5`, `< 0.5`) instead of comparing winner metrics against runner-up metrics, so persisted reason codes can misattribute why the winner actually won.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-orchestration skill procedure requires running BurnBarRunServiceTests, BurnBarMissionControlServiceTests, and BurnBarProviderRouterTests with explicit evidence, but this handoff recorded only BurnBarMissionControlServiceTests and OpenBurnBarCore tests while still marking `followedProcedure: true`.
Mission guidance says cross-surface assertions need artifacts from all participating surfaces, but VAL-CROSS-006 evidence in this run is daemon-only and omits app/extension validation commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/albertonunez/.factory/missions/d1d1287f-f2a9-4a34-8ba9-fce37df13493/AGENTS.md:30; features.json VAL-CROSS-006 verification steps include app test command at lines 1825-1830; handoff verification commands only include daemon/core tests (lines 16-23).

summary: Review failed due to two blocking reconciler correctness issues in winner selection candidate scope and sequence tie-break semantics. Additional shared-state observations were recorded for skill-procedure adherence and cross-surface validation evidence gaps.
